[2020-04-26 19:12] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): has the following tournament structure been proposed/tried in this game?

Play a best of two to get 1 point:

- First game is a random set/random start 
- Second game is the same set and the loser gets to pick if they go first or not
- Third game, same as second game (not needed if someone already got two wins).

Repeat until someone has 2 points (or whatever amount is agreed upon)

[2020-04-30 04:10] flakmaniak: I haven't played Runeterra, but my guess is the major digital card game designers are basically just shifting in the Hearthstone direction of "lots of randomness, little control" for more casual appeal or whatever. You CAN have high-randomness games that are also super high-skill and give an enormous edge to the better player. But I don't think these digital card games usually want to move that way, for whatever reason.
[2020-04-30 04:11] flakmaniak: I mean obviously there's Netrunner, which has a lot of outright randomness and variance, and lots of "it all comes down to this" moments where you're last-ditch deep digging in R&D or whatever. But Netrunner is very deep and high-skill and rewards creative and clever play and improvisation.
[2020-04-30 04:11] flakmaniak: Or for a different example, Galaxy Trucker has piles upon piles upon piles of randomness. But the best player at the table will win very, very consistently.
[2020-04-30 04:12] flakmaniak: Even if you ignore the randomness in-flight, and just look at the ship-building phase (where the real skill comes anyway), what tiles you draw is obviously super random. And this forces you to make tons of on-the-fly decisions. It adds and emphasizes skill because you're constantly confronted with new things you couldn't have calculated about.
[2020-04-30 04:12] flakmaniak: But the amount that that randomness can screw you over is pretty low, I think.
[2020-04-30 04:13] flakmaniak: Sure, you COULD draw the perfect tile that makes placing it really easy.
[2020-04-30 04:13] flakmaniak: Or you could draw some inconvenient and janky-looking tiles, and now have to figure out the puzzle of how to do something decent with them.
[2020-04-30 04:13] flakmaniak: Or the ever-difficult decision of whether to put on some crappy tiles or to keep digging.

messages.author: flakmaniak
messages.content: Or the ever-difficult decision of whether to put on some crappy tiles or to keep digging.
